entry.title: Re: Cost center MIS not available in system; manual Excel...
entry.content:
Hello @S0027059524 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, you are correct. A financial Statement Version is a GL account hierarchy used for financial statement presentation. You cannot add a CO dimension like Cost Center as an additional grouping level inside the same FSV structure.

Question: Is there any standard capability in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to enable Cost Center based grouping within an FSV structure?

Answer:

  • Cost center based grouping inside an FSV is not supported as a standard behaviour.
  • FSV like reporting with Cost Center drilldown is only supported when you use Embedded Analytics (Custom Analytical Queries, Manage KPIs and Reports and standard cost center apps), and optionally you may also use SAC for management reporting.

The following references provides some insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/8e040a45e4db4e6abd426bee0a86dc8a.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/c00cbf7fe8464663aee830fb6e7eec13.html

A Public Cloud, clean core best practice is to use a GL account hierarchy (your FSV) plus Cost Center as a characteristic in an analytical report.

Alternatively, if the ask is mainly from the P&L department, start with the delivered cost center analytics (Cost Centers Review Booklet) and cost accounting line item app (Display Line Items Cost Accounting), then align the layout to your business needs.

For more details, consult https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/95063eff5ee0467f8cc04b4ff8f8ca01.html

If the business wants an MIS pack that looks like an FSV that has multiple pages, commentary, scheduling and distribution, then the usual best practice is SAC consuming your S4H analytical query, your GL hierarchy and cost center hierarchy. See Custom Analytical Queries and KBA 2710858 for more insights.

In summary,

  • FSV is a GL account hierarchy and does not supporting adding cost center as an additional grouping level inside the FSV itself.
  • In Public Cloud, the best practice for an FSV like MIS with cost center drilldown is Embedded Analytics; where you maintain the GL hierarchy in Manage Global Hierarchies, build a Custom Analytical Query that includes GL account and cost center, and publish the statement style report in Manage KPIs and reports.
  • For executive packs, consume the same query in SAC.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP TM Transportation Allocation - Carrier 1 in the morning Carrier 2 in the afternoon
entry.content:
Dear TM Experts,

I am struggeling with the transportation allocation set up.

  • Carrier 1 (2010054) drives between 3am and 1pm.
  • Carrier 2 (2010051) drives between 2pm and 11pm.

I have therefore set up transportation allocation and changed the carrier selection settings in the planning profile to "use transportation allocation".

Nevertheless, it always picks 2010051, and gives this carrier 2 priority, although there is no priority given in the transportation lane. Any hint?

There are no good documentations found for transportation allocation, I assume there are not a lot of customers using it.

---

Set Up:

Carrier Selection Setting, see attachment 1

Transportation Allocation, see attachment 2

Transportation Lane, see attachment 3

SE38: /SCMTMS/TSPS_OPT_BGD, see attachment 4

I execute the tsps report in order to check the results - and the result is that in both cases it picks Carrier 2 (2010051) - why??! 

FO 6100336289 departure details:  03.03.2026 // 10:00 and arrival details: 03.03.2026 // 10:00

Thanks a lot in advance

Elena

check the attachments

entry.title: Re: Material Description 2
entry.content:
Hi  Ravi,
About material description data exactly we have 2 description but what is the difference between it.

in table MAKT u can see 2 description and the Purpose of them:

MAKTX is typically used for printing/displaying, while MAKTG is used for search help to avoid issues with inconsistent letter casing

  • MAKTX (Material Description): Stores the text as it is entered, supporting both upper and lower case letters.
  • MAKTG (Material Description in Upper Case): Automatically stores the uppercase version of MAKTX It is primarily used for consistent search/match code functionality.
  • I hope this help u
  • Regards.
  • Osama Selim

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC Cloud (ECC)
entry.content:
TLDR: Change service plan to Test and make sure Communication setting is configured correctly
We experienced the same issue, but have just resolved the issue.
We had a mismatch of our DRC Service Plan/Communication Setting. 
We wanted to set up a Test-system to send Customer Invoices from the business system through DRC, where we encountered the IFLOW_HTTP_COMMAND error. When we encountered the error, we had the service plan, Standard, and had set our Communication Settings to Test. 
We resolved it by changing the DRC service plan to Test aswell, which ultimately led to us being able to getting an approval from KSeF. However, currently we still experience some 403-error on the DRC error log.

entry.title: Migrating from Accelerator to Composable Storefront: A Technical Roadmap for Enterprises
entry.content:
If you're reading this, you're probably wondering whether it's time to move from your SAP Commerce Accelerator to Composable Storefront. Maybe your front-end team is frustrated with JSP templates, or you need better mobile experience. Let me walk you through what this migration actually looks like, based on real experience.

Why Migrate?

What you're leaving behind:

  • Server-side JSP rendering
  • Tightly coupled front-end and back-end
  • Limited mobile experience

What you're getting:

  • Modern Angular-based SPA
  • Decoupled architecture via OCC APIs
  • Better performance with SSR
  • Independent front-end deployment

The catch? You're moving from monolithic to microservices-style architecture. Your team needs JavaScript/TypeScript skills.

Step 1: Assessment

Start by cataloging your customizations:

# Find custom JSP files
find web/webroot/WEB-INF/views -name "*.jsp" | grep -v "\/yacceleratorstorefront\/"

# Custom controllers
grep -r "@Controller" --include="*Controller.java" | grep -v "de.hybris.platform"

Create a spreadsheet with every customization. This becomes your migration backlog.

Step 2: Build OCC Foundation

You need to expose everything via OCC APIs first. Here's a quick example for custom product data:

// Custom WsDTO
@ApiModel(value = "customProduct")
public class CustomProductWsDTO extends ProductWsDTO {
    private String warrantyInfo;
    private String installationGuideUrl;
    // getters/setters
}

// Populator
public class CustomProductPopulator implements Populator<ProductModel, CustomProductWsDTO> {
    @Override
    public void populate(ProductModel source, CustomProductWsDTO target) {
        target.setWarrantyInfo(source.getWarrantyInfo());
        target.setInstallationGuideUrl(source.getInstallationGuideUrl());
    }
}

For custom business logic, create OCC endpoints:

@Controller
@RequestMapping(value = "/{baseSiteId}/custom")
public class CustomOccController {
    
    @GetMapping("/products/{productCode}/compatibility")
    @ResponseBody
    public CompatibilityWsDTO checkCompatibility(
            @PathVariable String productCode,
            @RequestParam String customerProductId) {
        
        CompatibilityData data = customProductFacade
            .checkProductCompatibility(productCode, customerProductId);
        return dataMapper.map(data, CompatibilityWsDTO.class);
    }
}

Step 3: Setup Composable Storefront

# Install and setup
ng new my-storefront
cd my-storefront
ng add @spartacus/schematics --base-site=electronics --base-url=https://localhost:9002

Configure your endpoints:

// spartacus-configuration.module.ts
provideConfig({
  backend: {
    occ: {
      baseUrl: environment.occBaseUrl,
      prefix: '/occ/v2/',
      endpoints: {
        product: 'products/${productCode}?fields=DEFAULT,warrantyInfo',
        productCompatibility: 'custom/products/${productCode}/compatibility'
      }
    }
  },
  context: {
    baseSite: ['electronics'],
    currency: ['USD'],
    language: ['en']
  }
})

Step 4: Migrate Components

Here's a real migration example.

Old Accelerator JSP:

<div class="product-detail">
    <h1>${product.name}</h1>
    <div class="warranty-info">
        <strong>Warranty:</strong> ${product.warrantyInfo}
    </div>
</div>

New Angular Component:

// custom-product-detail.component.ts
@Component({
  selector: 'app-custom-product-detail',
  templateUrl: './custom-product-detail.component.html'
})
export class CustomProductDetailComponent implements OnInit {
  product$: Observable<CustomProduct>;
  
  constructor(
    private currentProductService: CurrentProductService,
    private customProductService: CustomProductService
  ) {}
  
  ngOnInit(): void {
    this.product$ = this.customProductService.getCustomProduct();
  }
}
<!-- custom-product-detail.component.html -->
<div *ngIf="product$ | async as product">
  <h1>{{ product.name }}</h1>
  <div *ngIf="product.warrantyInfo">
    <strong>{{ 'product.warranty' | cxTranslate }}:</strong>
    <p>{{ product.warrantyInfo }}</p>
  </div>
</div>

Replace default Spartacus components via config:

provideConfig({
  cmsComponents: {
    ProductDetailsTabComponent: {
      component: CustomProductDetailComponent
    }
  }
})

Step 5: Checkout

Adding custom checkout steps is straightforward:

// Custom step configuration
export const customCheckoutSteps = [
  {
    id: 'deliveryAddress',
    name: 'checkoutProgress.deliveryAddress',
    routeName: 'checkoutDeliveryAddress',
    type: [CheckoutStepType.DELIVERY_ADDRESS]
  },
  {
    id: 'giftMessage',  // Your custom step
    name: 'checkoutProgress.giftMessage',
    routeName: 'checkoutGiftMessage',
    type: [CheckoutStepType.PAYMENT_DETAILS]
  },
  // ... more steps
];

Step 6: SSR Setup

ng add @spartacus/setup --ssr

Add caching for better performance:

// server.ts
const cache = new LRU({
  max: 100,
  ttl: 1000 * 60 * 60 // 1 hour
});

app.engine('html', (filePath, options, callback) => {
  const cacheKey = options.req.url;
  const cachedHtml = cache.get(cacheKey);
  
  if (cachedHtml) {
    return callback(null, cachedHtml);
  }
  
  ngExpressEngine({ bootstrap: AppServerModule })(filePath, options, (err, html) => {
    if (!err && html) cache.set(cacheKey, html);
    callback(err, html);
  });
});

Step 7: Deploy

npm run build:ssr

Simple Kubernetes deployment:

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: composable-storefront
spec:
  replicas: 3
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: storefront-ssr
        image: your-registry/composable-storefront:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 4000
        env:
        - name: OCC_BASE_URL
          value: "https://your-commerce-backend.com"

Common Issues & Quick Fixes

CORS errors?

# local.properties
corsfilter.commercewebservices.allowedOrigins=http://localhost:4200

Random logouts?

provideConfig({
  session: { synchronization: true }
})

Translations not working? Structure your i18n files properly:

// assets/i18n/en/product.json
{
  "product": {
    "warranty": "Warranty",
    "downloadGuide": "Download Guide"
  }
}

My Advice

Don't try to migrate everything at once. Start with one section, test thoroughly, then expand. The Composable Storefront is actively maintained by SAP, so you're not just modernizing—you're joining an ecosystem that keeps evolving.

The migration is substantial but worth it. Your team gets modern tooling, faster deployments, and better customer experience.

DISCLAIMER - This is the process according to my experience which I have done in the past, this is not a official documentation.

entry.title: snc/accept_insecure_rfc
entry.content:
Dear All,

I wanted to share information with you all on the parameter "snc/accept_insecure_rfc"

Possible values are 0, 1 and U

0 - Reject unprotected external RFCs (internal RFCs are either accepted or rejected depending on the parameter snc/accept_insecure_r3int_rfc)

1 - Accept all unprotected RFCs (internal and external)

U - Accept unprotected external RFCs for those users who have the appropriate flag set in their user master record (see User Maintenance on AS ABAP)

now if you are planning to set it to 'U' then you might not be aware what is the flag which need to be set.

 

Answer > su01 >> user >> SNC >> SNC Data >> Allow password logon for SAP GUI(user-specific)

Thank you

Rohan

entry.title: Efficient Handover of Recipe Formulations with SAP Integrated Product Development
entry.content:
Welcome to this blog post! In today’s edition, I want to outline how you can efficiently hand over recipe formulations to manufacturing BOMs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD).

No time for reading? Listen in on our latest Meet the Expert session on the topic.

SAP IPD allows production engineers to derive and maintain bills of material (BOMs) from the recipe formulation handover application in SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD) into an external application like SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Learn how to effectively manage product structures, saving material and operational costs in the process!

In the handover scenario we want to look at today, a recipe engineer has created a formulation recipe in SAP IPD and wants to hand it over to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for production. SAP IPD supports the whole process of referencing a source formulation recipe and deriving a manufacturing BOM out of it.

Recipe engineers can use simplified planning options to plan and restructure BOM items, synchronize the target manufacturing BOM and enrich it with relevant details, like subsets of BOM item attributes.

Production engineers can enjoy our user-friendly Change Incorporation feature to compare and incorporate changes initiated in the SAP IPD recipe formulation to the target BOM, including adding new ingredients that were not included before and updating quantities.

Such changes can be made with or without an existing change number, but always ensuring the target BOM reflects the latest recipe. After you have incorporated all changes into the target BOM, the Change Reconciliation icon will let you know in case there are discrepancies between the source and the target panels and provide status information.

Click on the icon to open the Change Reconciliation Workbench and easily reconcile the changes, ensuring the manufacturing BOM remains accurate and up-to-date, for optimum production consistency and quality.

The application also allows you to add certain manufacturing consumables and other essential details to the BOM, such as item texts and scrap material. This enrichment brings the BOM closer to the production-ready status, reducing the transition time and effort from R&D to production and improving efficiency.

Sounds good? Also want to know what’s next for Process Industry customers?

There are good reasons to look forward to our coming releases:

Among the features we plan to bring to you soon are the smart production preparation for target BOMs, AI-based mass creation of handover activities for different plants, improved handover to the master recipe, and the background creation of handover activities. So stay tuned on our Roadmap for more streamlined processes with optimal consistency and quality.

As you can see, we are all set for a successful start to 2026.

For more details about the handover of recipe formulations to manufacturing BOMs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, including FAQs, check out our latest Meet the Expert session on the topic.

More Information / Additional Links 

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts here:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: linkedin.com/in/annette-garcia-lorenzo

entry.title: 🇧🇷 SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications: ML, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs (Ribeirão Preto, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event focuses on SAP HANA Cloud for AI Applications, covering topics such as machine learning, Vectors, and Knowledge Graphs. You don't need to have any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity and some basic programming skills!

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Friday, April 10, in Ribeirão Preto, Brazil 🇧🇷 from 09:30 to 14:30 local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

The event is at Fatec Ribeirão Preto - Av. Pio XII, 1255 - Vila Virginia, Ribeirão Preto - State of São Paulo, Brazil

Please note that space is limited, as we can only accommodate 25 attendees.

By the end of the session, you’ll have built examples that demonstrate how SAP HANA Cloud can serve as a versatile back-end for modern Artificial Intelligence applications:

  • Leverage the SAP HANA Vector Engine for high‑dimensional embeddings—building a vector store that supports semantic similarity search.
  • Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)  to enrich generative AI outputs with precise, up-to-date data.
  • Model and Query a Knowledge Graph inside SAP HANA using RDF triples to extract semantic relationships.
  • Integrate HANA with LangChain to build AI apps that orchestrate prompt workflows, execute database statements, and navigate knowledge graphs—all directly from Python, in this case.

entry.title: Re: Texts in Purchase Contract Output Form not Displayed
entry.content:
Dear @SauMaris,

The most common miss in your specific case is wrong Text IDs (A** vs K**)

Text IDs with prefix A are typically used for RFQs, while contracts use K**. Community references explicitly describe the convention F = PO, A = RFQ, K = Contract so if you configured A05 / A06 in CBC ID 104689 the output framework will not find matching contract texts and TextNodes stay empty in the form data source

More details below: (I know the user is writing about the Private Cloud version but it is referring to the Public Cloud version, screenshot from my CBC for the Public Cloud version)

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/po-text-id/qaq-p/12768369

Summary:

In CBC 104689 Define Texts for Contract, maintain:

  • Header texts tab:
    • Text object = EKKO
    • Text ID = K05 (Terms of Delivery) / K06 (Shipping Instructions) (not A05/A06)

 

  • Item texts tab:
    • Text object = EKPO
    • relevant K** text IDs used at item level

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: New JavaScript Event in SAP Analytics Cloud End User File Upload
entry.content:
Hey @gerd_schoeffl,

This is a good update,

But, I don't know what is use case of onBeforeExecute, because just get called when you click the Upload Button. It should be called after selecting file would be more helpful.

Example: - If someone want to apply some validation, like i want to show popup message to user after selecting file, and that time I will show this "XYZ ACCOUNT members are not allowed" using Popup. and then there should be one more function called as DataUploadStarter_1.execute().

So in the Popup we will write script on button.

OK, Continue --> DataUploadStarter_1.execute()
CANCEL --> Close the Popup

I know we can do this in onBeforeImport, to skip that account which are not allowed. But user won't know which account are ignored. We can show that in onAfterExecute. But it would be good if we can show some validation or any message before Upload (after file selected).

Hi,

Maybe too late to answer, but you can also do a rebind of the table:

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Build code-based AI Agents on SAP Business Technology Platform (São Leopoldo, Brazil)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam, you will learn how to build state-of-the-art AI agents using Generative AI Hub, Python and JavaScript. You will also gain the skills to create custom tools for your agents, including leveraging SAP's RPT-1 model. Finally, you will deploy your agents to BTP.

In this CodeJam you can choose to build your agents with Python, CrewAI and LiteLLM or with JavaScript, LangGraph and LiteLLM.

The CodeJam will include the following SAP technologies:
- Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
- SAP RPT-1
- Grounding Service
- SAP AI Launchpad
- Business Application Studio (Python, JavaScript)

code-based agents w. JavaScript

code-based agents w. Python

The event is at SAP Labs Latin America (SLE01) - Avenida SAP - Cristo Rei, São Leopoldo - RS, Brazil.

entry.title: Re: Resource not shown in the PurReq in SAP APO
entry.content:
Troubleshooting: Resource Not Displayed in APO Purchase Requisition for Subcontracting

Symptom

  • Resource is not displayed in the Purchase Requisition product view despite being configured in the transportation lane
  • Resource absence prevents visibility in the planning board [/SAPAPO/CDPS0]
  • Purchase Requisition is visible in product view but lacks resource information
  • Planning board does not show the subcontracting resource for capacity planning

Current System Setup

  • Scenario Type: Subcontracting with vendor-to-plant transportation lane
  • Transportation Lane: Configured from vendor to receiving plant with SNP type resource
  • Resource Configuration: SNP type resource assigned to transportation lane
  • Transaction: [/SAPAPO/CDPS0] shows Purchase Requisition but missing resource details

Possible Root Causes

  1. Resource Master Data Issues: Resource not properly created or assigned to correct model in APO 
  2. Purchase Requisition Item Category: Missing item category "L" for subcontracting in Purchase Requisition 
  3. Transportation Lane Configuration: Incomplete means of transport or resource assignment in T-lane 
  4. External Procurement Relationship: Subcontracting external procurement relationship not properly configured 
  5. Product Master Configuration: Missing product master records in subcontractor location 

Diagnostic Steps

  1. Verify Resource Master Data: Check if resource exists in APO using /SAPAPO/RESNET and ensure it's assigned to model 000 
  2. Check Purchase Requisition Parameters: Verify item category is set to "L" for subcontracting and source of supply is correctly assigned
  3. Validate Transportation Lane: Confirm T-lane has proper means of transport and resource assignment using /SAPAPO/SCC_TL1
  4. Review External Procurement Relationship: Ensure subcontracting external procurement relationship exists and is properly configured
  5. Analyze Product View Configuration: Check if product masters exist in subcontractor location and are assigned to model 000 

Resolution Steps

  1. Configure Resource Assignment: Ensure resource is created in APO and assigned to model 000 using /SAPAPO/RESNET
  2. Update Purchase Requisition Mapping: Verify external procurement relationship has subcontracting category and proper item category "L" 
  3. Complete Transportation Lane Setup: Assign appropriate means of transport and ensure resource is properly linked to T-lane
  4. Create Missing Product Masters: Create end product and components in subcontractor location and assign to model 000
  5. Refresh Planning Data: Re-run SNP planning to regenerate Purchase Requisitions with correct resource assignments

Validation Checklist

  • Resource appears in Purchase Requisition product view with proper assignment
  • Resource visible in planning board [/SAPAPO/CDPS0] for capacity planning
  • Transportation lane properly configured with means of transport and resource
  • Purchase Requisition has item category "L" for subcontracting scenario
  • External procurement relationship shows subcontracting category
  • Product masters exist in subcontractor location assigned to model 000

Configuration Areas to Review

Component Configuration Point Expected Setting
Resource Master/SAPAPO/RESNETResource assigned to model 000
Purchase Req MappingItem Category"L" for subcontracting
Transportation LaneMeans of TransportProper resource assignment
External ProcurementCategorySubcontracting type
Product MasterSubcontractor LocationAssigned to model 000
Purchase Info RecordSubcontracting TypeProduction version specified

Preventive Measures

  • Regular validation of resource master data assignments to ensure model 000 assignment
  • Monitoring of transportation lane configurations for complete means of transport setup
  • Systematic verification of external procurement relationships for subcontracting scenarios
  • Periodic review of product master data in subcontractor locations

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity
entry.content:
Why SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity Matters

What Is SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC)?

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is a secure, SAP-owned and managed cloud service that connects SAP ERP systems directly to banks and financial institutions.

Figure

It enables the automated exchange of:

  • Payment files
  • Bank statements
  • Acknowledgement messages
  • Treasury and cash management data

In short: MBC acts as a single digital gateway between your ERP and all your banks.

Key Benefits & Features

Why do customers implement MBC?

  • One centralized connection to multiple banks
  • Increased transparency and automation
  • Secure communication via SWIFT, EBICS, SFTP, APIs
  • Automated processing of payments and bank statements
  • Direct integration with payment approval workflows

For treasury and finance teams, this means greater control and fewer manual touchpoints.

Ready for Global Banking Formats

One of the strengths of MBC is its ability to handle global standards.

Supported Formats Include: ISO 20022: PAIN.001 / PAIN.002 / PAIN.008, CAMT.052 / CAMT.053 / CAMT.054; SWIFT MT: MT101, MT940, MT300 / MT320.

This flexibility makes it suitable for both regional and global banking landscapes.

Who Can Benefit?

MBC is available for:

  • SAP ERP ECC (EHP0 and higher)
  • SAP S/4HANA On-Premise
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public & Private Editions)

One Solution – Multiple Connection Options

The connection model depends on your banking landscape. You can combine: SWIFT,  SFTP (Host-to-Host), EBICS, APIs, Member Bank integration

Figure

💡Mixed scenarios are supported — for example, using member bank connections for some banks and SFTP for others.

Alignment with your bank should happen early in the project.

How Much Does It Cost?

Pricing details are available on request via the SAP Product Page.

License Types:

Base Connection PackageAdvanced Connection Package

Up to 5 customer-nominated bank connections

Member banks not counted

1 productive + 1 test tenant

Unlimited bank connections

Includes SWIFT membership

1 productive + 1 test tenant

Selecting the right license depends on your number of banks and global complexity.

Getting Started: First Steps

Before anything else:

  • Active MBC license
  • Alignment with banks on file formats and connectivity type
  • ERP system ready for integration

Early coordination with financial institutions avoids delays later.

Onboarding Resource Center

The SAP Onboarding Resource Center delivers organized, solution-specific content to guide you through every step of your onboarding journey. I highly recommended to review the MBC onboarding materials.

Roles and Responsibilities

The MBC implementation requires clarity across:

  • ERP configuration team
  • SAP MBC administrators
  • Bank integration contacts
  • Treasury / finance stakeholders

Clear roles and responsabilities avoids bottlenecks.

Figure

Provisioning the MBC Tenant

Once the contract is signed:

  1. The IT contact receives an S-User with Cloud Administrator authorization for MBC.
  2. An MBC onboarding email is sent  to the designated IT contact on contract start date.
  3. The MBC tenant becomes available in SAP for Me.

Customer Activities:

  • Cloud Administrator initiates MBC provisioning in SAP for Me
    • Select data center (all data centers are currently on amazon webservices)
  • Assign MBC access to implementation team

After submission, confirmation is sent via email. System details are visible in SAP for Me.

Connecting ERP to MBC

Connecting your ERP system involves configuration to your setup. You can find step-by-step guidance in the SAP Help Portal  ERP to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, getting connected is quick and easy, taking just a few simple steps.

Figure

Configure Your ERP to the MBC Connector

The MBC connector acts as the bridge between ERP and MBC. The configuration steps vary depending on the ERP system in use. Detailed instructions for each ERP are available in the SAP Help Portal under ERP to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

All inbound and outbound messages pass through the MBC connector.

Outbound Messages (ERP → Bank)Inbound Messages (Bank → ERP)
  • No physical file is generated
  • The connector creates a structured message
  • The message is sent directly to MBC
  • MBC forwards it securely to the bank
  • Received via web service (push or pull)
  • Decrypted
  • Signature-verified
  • Stored in inbound message database
  • Processed automatically or flagged for manual review

Connectivity Type: MBC to Bank/FSI

It’s important to align with the bank on the MBC project before it begins, ensuring that the connection type is agreed upon in advance.

Figure

🔔For SFTP, EBICS, or Member Bank Integration:

Each bank must complete a connection template including:

  • Server settings
  • File formats
  • IP ranges

Required Steps:

  1. Download bank connection template (S-User required)
  2. Complete template
  3. Submit Service Request in SAP for Me (Attach template)
  4. SAP MBC team will proceed with the integration tasks

🔔For SWIFT Integration

Initiate Swift Onboarding:

  1. Notify SWIFT (you can find the detailed information hier)
  2. Provide company name and location
  3. Await SWIFT account management contact
  4. Complete BIC migration if required
  5. Submit Service Request in SAP for Me
  6. SAP MBC team will proceed with the integration tasks

Final Thoughts

If you're starting your MBC journey, focus on:

  • Early bank alignment
  • Clear role definition
  • Proper connector configuration

 And most importantly — use the SAP Community.

Further Reading:

MBC Product Overview SAPSAP Multi-Bank Connectivity
MBC

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

Everything you need to know to deploy, configure and operate SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity
Connector for MBC

Connector for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

Everything you need to know to install, configure, and operate connector for SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity
Blog SAP Community MBC Onboarding

Get started with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (Basis – Onboarding Process step-by-step)

Tip: !Watch the Webcast!
Blog SAP Community MBCSAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC) – All You Need to Know
MBC MicrolearningAccounts Receivables and Accounts Payables with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC) - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Finance
Blog SAP CommunitySAP S/4 HANA Cloud Essentials MBC Bank Payments Automation

entry.title: Re: Sorting in Table with compact display doesn't work
entry.content:
The sorting can be changed when compact display is disabled. When re-enabling compact display, it keeps the new sorting. 

Story developers can define the table sorting (dimension or measure) at design time with compact display off and then enabling the compact display. 

For end users (story consumers) there is a practical work around without coding since the latest Q1.2026 SAC release: Enable the new "Quick Builder" option for the table. Now the end user can disable compact display, change the table sorting and re-enable the compact display during story consumption.

entry.title: Re: Bank transactions going to wrong account -error "Acco...
entry.content:
Hello @kezelda

I meant you should make the automatic matching logic more specific so the system cannot confuse a customer (D) with a supplier (K) when interpreting bank statement text. See KBA 3609627 for more details. You can do this in:

The Manage Processing Rules for Bank Statements app to create rules like Memo Line contains... (the tightening means to reduce overlap between patterns that could match both AR and AP).

See KBA 3431169

Here is a quick guide:

  • In Reprocess Bank Statement Items app, open one incorrectly allocated item and evaluate the fields in the line details.

  • Create a more specific rule in the Manage Processing Rules for Bank Statements. You can either create or adjust a rule for your relevant scenario. Make sure you use Memo Line à Operator Contains and add additional conditions where possible, so you avoid collisions for example with bank account, external transaction type, among sign, etc. KBA 3609627 describes the supported approach for search string like behaviour. 

  • Then retest in Reprocess Bank Statement Items app with the same bank statement item

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC): A Beginner's Guide - Part 1
entry.content: @felipesoares  For BW and BW/4HANA professionals, the best way to study BDC is to start with SAP’s BDC Learning Journeys and then get hands‑on with data products and semantic modeling, since these are the modern replacements for BW objects.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC): A Beginner's Guide - Part 1
entry.content: @Sanjay_Barnwal  BDC doesn’t expose raw S/4HANA tables like LFA1 or ACDOCA; instead, the data is organized into business entities and data products that represent those tables in a unified semantic model, so you work with harmonized business objects rather than technical table names.

entry.title: Re: Download iFlow with integration content API without merging configured values with defaults
entry.content:

However, the API for downloading a whole package delivers the IFlows with the default, not the configured, parameters. But you need to extract the files ending with "_content" and rename them as .zip, 

You get the package like this: https://{{apihost}}/api/v1/IntegrationPackages('{{PackageName}}')/$value

Philippe

entry.title: SAP Commodity Management: Driving the Bottom Line with Precision Expense Control
entry.content:
In commodity trading, third-party expenses are not merely overheads; they are critical factors that significantly influence the profitability of trading activities. Costs such as demurrage, brokerage, throughput charges, insurance, and tank cleaning, when not managed precisely, can lead to margin and revenue leakage. While SAP’s native capabilities offer robust expense management, the unique and dynamic requirements of commodity supply chains, including the need to track costs throughout the entire deal and contract lifecycle, necessitate a specialized approach.

To address these industry-specific challenges, we are thrilled to have launched SAP Commodity Expense Management, as part of S/4HANA 2025. This new product is purpose-built as an application on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), designed specifically for commodity organizations to provide focused, industry-relevant expense management, all supported by dedicated and intuitive Fiori Apps delivering enhanced usability.

The solution delivers several key benefits:

  • Increased Automation: Leverages automation rules defined in Expense Master Data, automatic defaulting of expenses during planning and automated accrual reversals and adjustments.
  • Enhanced Integration and Visibility: Provides cross-company visibility of expenses across multiple document types from capture to settlement.
  • Improved Data Accuracy: Reduces the risk of margin and revenue leakage through better expense planning and integrated reporting.

SAP Commodity Expense Management offers a comprehensive suite of capabilities, ensuring granular control and enhanced visibility over commodity-related costs across both Energy and Agri industries:

  • Expense Setup & Configuration: This feature provides tools to define the fundamental structure of your expenses. It allows for the setup of various expense types, units of measure (UoMs), currencies, and account mapping. Users can configure automation rules, mass update expense rates, and maintain essential posting assignments for both accrual and settlement processes. Furthermore, the solution offers extensibility with flexibility to define attributes for determining templates and expense rates, along with APIs for mass upload of these rates.
  • Expense Planning: Essential for proactive cost management, this capability supports both automatic and manual expense planning. Expenses can be planned during deal capture, contract creation, nomination, and Agricultural Contract Management  Application Document items, allowing for iterative expense updates throughout the process. The system facilitates defaulting of expenses based on predefined automation rules and allows tracking of overrides and manual additions at various stages. It offers the ability to determine expenses using customer-specific attributes during Deal Capture.
  • Expense Accrual: This function handles the recognition of liabilities for services received. Accruals can be created and adjusted based on key events such as Deal Publish, Contract Approval, Nomination Confirmation, and ACM Application Documents/Ticketing. It includes features for automatic accrual reversals, automatic accrual on ticket corrections, and the ability to split and collate accruals for complex scenarios. Accrual postings are integrated during delivery execution.
  • Expense Settlement: Designed to streamline the payment process, this feature supports invoice verification and the settlement of both planned and unplanned expenses, distinct from core commodity contract settlements. It allows for correction of service provider and invoice amounts and provides for the recovery of expenses from contract or deal counterparties. An API is available to capture Expense Settlement, and it includes tax code determination for accurate financial postings. Settlement postings are integrated into the accounting process.

By bringing precision, automation, and comprehensive visibility to third-party costs, SAP Commodity Expense Management empowers organizations to transform expenses from a potential liability into a strategic advantage. Don’t let unmanaged costs erode your profitability; embrace the future of commodity expense control. We invite you to explore the capabilities further by visiting the SAP Help Portal and Roadmap Explorer (links below).

Visit SAP Help Portal for more an Administration Guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/CCS/7b6b503b6ae0446fb3d6801c98f56b1e/2fe3a2ae04e74a598c8c39f73b4710e3.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

Stay tuned for future innovations by following SAP Roadmap Explorer  for Commodity Management: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=42F2E964F4E71EDAA6A3587248CD40E5&range=CURRENT-LAST

Post 2H 2023 Release,  for any new SSO integrations, it is mandatory to have IAS in place as SSO provider. Now you can only use any third party IDP as corporate IDP via IAS. IAS is now a mandatory for the instances SSO set up and the add button for new direct integration between SuccessFactors - third party IDP has also been removed in 2023 as explained on KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3422297. The direct integration of corporate IDP to SuccessFactors will no longer be supported after June 2025 and will be deprecated in November 2026. Cristian

entry.title: Re: Intercompany Order-Related Billing (Output document R...
entry.content:
Hi @Alenin_Kirill ,
My friend, the intercompany credit‑memo flows in Advanced Intercompany Sales are one of those areas where SAP gives you half the automation (RDIV → IC invoice/credit memo) but leaves the vendor‑side posting to project design. What you’re running into is a known limitation. RDIV (RVIVAUFT) only creates the FI vendor invoice/credit memo when there is an underlying PO history.
In your refund scenario, there is no PO, so RD04 cannot work, and RDIV only posts the customer‑side IC credit memo (IGA) in the delivering company.

You can do following option to automate vendor credit memo.

Option 1.
This is the cleanest and most SAP‑aligned solution.
Create a PO automatically when the customer credit memo is created (BAdI or output‑triggered).
Use RD04 to generate the vendor credit memo. PO is never used for logistics—only for FI integration.
But this will requires development to auto‑create PO and also Adds an extra document in the flow.

Option 2.
You can enhance RVIVAUFT via:
BAdI: BADI_SD_BILLING
User exit: EXIT_SAPLV60B_008 (output processing)
BTE 1650 (FI document creation)
The Logic is to detect RDIV output for credit memo. Read the IC billing document. Create FI vendor credit memo via BAPI: BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST or BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_CREATE. This will be fully automated, No PO required and Cleanest user experience.

Hope this will help you. Let me know which option work with you fine. if it work please accept the solution.

entry.title: Re: User ID Change
entry.content:
Hi A,

 

You are correct, the currently available process is described on 2392253 and involves purging the user. 

Since User ID is unique number in the system for a user and can’t be changed. Once this value has been used, it can’t be reused. Its sole purpose is to be the unique identifier for the person's employment in the system and is mapped from the Person ID for Employee Central customers.

  • If a concurrent employment is added to the employee, then the system automatically assigns another username and user ID to the employee.
  • This can be used for single sign-on (SSO) but it is not recommended.

If you would like to have the employee with different User ID then it is recommended you take a dump/bakup of employee data, i.e. all personal, job info and others and terminate previous ID as seen here:2317955

Follow order below and 2545988 - How to Purge/Delete users in SuccessFactors

Step 1: Run a report as seen here: 2317955

Step 2:Terminate User Via: MSS - UI or Take action - terminate

Step 3: Soft: Purge

  1. Navigate to Admin Tools -> Data Retention Management ->  Create New Purge Request
  2. Select Purge Inactive User
  3. Upload a list of users (by User ID or Assignment ID). 

Step 4:  3058601 - How To Check Soft or Permanent Purge User

Step 5: 2392076 - User Permanent or Hard Purge feature  (2749427 - Unable To Permanently Purge user after a Soft Purge)

User Permanent Purge

  • To permanently purge user follow:
    1. Navigate to Admin Center > Data Retention Management > System Identifier Purge.
    2. Purge inactive users listed in a CSV file
    3. Use: user ID or assignment ID in a CSV fil

Hire with desired User ID (you can do hire via import too) and add backed up data from 2317955  to create the employee data again.

This would be similar as if we are adding another user but will have all the data same as old one. This is the only safe and recommended way this requirement could be achieved.

 

Kind Regards,

Cristian

entry.title: Re: Trading partner determination for PGI Material accoun...
entry.content:
Hi @Safal ,
The intercompany PGI trading‑partner issues almost always come down to how SAP determines the company code of the “counterparty” in the material document. In other words: Which company code does SAP think is on the other side of the intercompany movement? If that determination is wrong, the trading partner (VBUND) will also be wrong.

Following options can help you, depending on your business structure.

Option 1 — Correct the Group Company Assignment (OBY6)
Ensure both company codes involved in intercompany transactions belong to the correct group company.

Option 2 — Align Company Code Assignments
If the delivering plant is assigned to the wrong company code, correct OX10.

Option 3 — Validate Intercompany Setup
OVXA / OVX5 (sales org → company code)
OVX6 (plant → company code)
OVVA (internal customer for billing only)

Please accept the solution if any option work with you.

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC): A Beginner's Guide - Part 3
entry.content:
Introduction

This is an SAP blog series on SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC). This is the third part of the blog series SAP Business Data Cloud - A Beginner's Guide. If you missed the first and second part please go through that and let me know your feedback or comments. 

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC): A Beginner's Guide - Part 1 - In the first part, I covered Introduction to SAP Business Data Cloud, Why do we need SAP Business Data Cloud, and Key Components of SAP Business Data Cloud. 

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC): A Beginner's Guide - Part 2 - In the second part, I covered the Architecture of SAP Business Data Cloud. 

I hope you have got a basic understanding about SAP Business Data Cloud and Architecture of SAP Business Data Cloud .

In this part, I'm planning to cover what are the components of SAP Business Data Cloud and it's role in SAP BDC in beginner's language. 

SAP Datasphere

Let’s start with SAP Datasphere, which is the core data modeling and integration component of SAP Business Data Cloud.
Datasphere integrates and harmonizes data from SAP and non-SAP sources, and helps in ensuring raw data is structured and meaningful. Raw data alone isn’t sufficient for analytics or AI it needs context and semantic definitions.

SAP Datasphere offers options for semantic enrichment like classify datasets as facts, dimensions, hierarchies, or text, and specify field meanings such as date, currency, or unit, and also setting aggregation rules like sum, average, min, or max. and it converts raw data into business-ready, semantically enriched models.

Datasphere supports prebuilt models from SAP as well as custom models combining SAP-managed data with partner or customer data products. These models can be consumed by SAP Analytics Cloud or AI/ML workflows in Databricks.
In SAP BDC, Datasphere acts as the trusted foundation, bridging raw data from source systems to analytics and AI, ensuring consistency and semantic clarity across the platform.

SAP Databricks

Next, let’s discuss SAP Databricks, the AI and machine learning engine within BDC.
Databricks provides a collaborative platform for data scientists to develop and deploy AI/ML models on top of harmonized BDC data. It covers the entire ML lifecycle, from experimentation to production, including advanced cases like generative AI.

Data engineers can perform ingestion, transformation, and monitoring while Delta Share enables seamless, zero-copy access to BDC data products. This ensures AI/ML workflows operate on live, governed data without duplication.
In BDC, role of Databricks is to extend harmonized data products with intelligence, delivering predictive and actionable insights that can be visualized in Analytics Cloud or reused in other BDC components.

SAP BW

Now let’s look at SAP BW integration. Many organizations already have valuable historical data in SAP BW or BW/4HANA, and BDC allows you to leverage this data in the cloud without disruption.
The integration follows three steps:

  1. Lift : to move your on-premise BW system to the BDC private cloud for extended maintenance and reduced infrastructure management.
  2. Shift : to generate data products from BW Info Providers so they can be consumed in Datasphere, Analytics Cloud, or Databricks.
  3. Innovate : to adopt SAP-managed data products and Intelligent Applications, and modernizing analytics workflows over time.

In BDC, BW provides historical, trusted enterprise data as ready-to-use data products, enabling AI/ML, dashboards, and custom analytics scenarios while preserving previous investments.

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

Next component is SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). SAC is the front-end of BDC.

It provides dashboards, charts, tables, maps, and stories for interactive exploration of harmonized and enriched data. Users can drill down, filter, and perform planning functions

Analytics Cloud integrates with Datasphere, Databricks, and BW models, so all visualizations are based on trusted, governed data. It also leverages AI/ML features like predictive insights and the ‘Just Ask’ natural language query assistant.
Analytics Cloud supports both prebuilt Intelligent Applications and custom dashboards, enabling users to turn enriched data and AI insights into actionable decisions.

Its role in BDC is to provide a unified interface for business users, helping them analyze, plan, and act on data efficiently.

Data Foundation Services

Finally, let’s discuss the Foundation Services of SAP Business Data Cloud.
Foundation Services manage SAP-managed data products: they replicate, harmonize, and store them in the SAP HANA Cloud Data Lake. The data is stored in highly efficient files rather than tables, enabling large-scale, high-performance read operations.
Foundation Services also support Delta Share, an open protocol that allows data to be shared securely with zero copying. Consumers such as SAP Datasphere, SAP Databricks, or other analytics tools can access data efficiently while maintaining governance.
Discovery of available data products is facilitated via Open Resource Discovery (ORD), which exposes technical definitions, metadata, and business descriptions. Users can explore products before generating or installing them.
In BDC, Foundation Services act as the backbone, ensuring that all SAP-managed data products are up-to-date, discoverable, and securely available for analytics, AI/ML, and Intelligent Applications. Without Foundation Services, data products and intelligent applications would not have a trusted, governed environment for reliable consumption.

 

Conclusion

In this blog we covered the components of SAP Business Data Cloud and it's role in SAP BDC. This is the part 3 of the SAP Business Data Cloud - A beginner's guide series.

In case if you missed the part 1 and 2, here is the links:

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC): A Beginner's Guide - Part 1 

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC): A Beginner's Guide - Part 2

If you have any feedback, questions or queries, please post them as comments. I will try to answer the maximum as per my knowledge.

entry.title: Re: FB50 Display only
entry.content:
Hi @i_only ,

My friend, It sounds like you’ve already set ACTVT = 03 (Display) for FB50, but the user can still post (save) documents. In SAP FI, this usually happens because FB50 does not control posting authorization by itself — posting is controlled by other authorization objects.

Please follow the steps to make FB50 display-only, you must remove posting activities from the posting objects.

Step-by-step
Go to PFCG → open the user’s role.

In Authorizations, find these objects:
F_BKPF_BUK
F_BKPF_KOA
Set ACTVT = 03 (Display) only.

Remove:
01 (Create)
02 (Change)
16 (Post)
Regenerate the role and assign it.

If this will help you fix the issue please accept my solution.

My friend, you cannot change the company code once the F110 proposal is created In standard SAP, the company code is fixed at the moment you run F110. You cannot switch it afterward inside the proposal.
So the only way to apply logic is to intercept the process before or during proposal creation using enhancements.

entry.title: Re: Receive supplier invoices from authorities' platform and send them to different automation solut
entry.content:
Hello,

I was just looking at the feasibility of this option and I must say I came to different conclusion. We are running S4HANA 2023 FS2  and the BADI is not called at all.

As per my findings, the button for Create Incoming Invoice (action INCOM_AUTO) only appears if we have customized the Assignment of an Automation Solution to a Process or Company Code (SM30: EDOINCOMSOLV). This code runs in CL_EDOC_SEL_TOOLBAR_UI->DEACTIVATE_INCOMING_ACTIONS and the BADI is not called. This Table has Process and Company Code as primary keys so we cannot have more than one combination.

When we click the button the 2nd BADI EDOC_INCOM_CONNECTOR is indeed called. This runs in method CL_EDOC_INCOM_CONNECTOR->GET_INSTANCE. But here, the Automation Solution was already assigned. The Get Instance method parameters needed for the 1st BADi to run are not even supplied. 

Please see attached files for code screenshots.

Case 220530/2026 was created for SAP.

entry.title: Re: Error: Purchase Requisition processing for Product ty...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your query regarding the Business Partner setup and reporting impact.

As per the standard design of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, when a supplier is assigned to a plant, the system treats it as an internal supplier for Stock Transport Order (STO) processes. In such cases, inventory validation becomes mandatory. Since service materials are not inventory-managed, the system prevents processing and triggers the related error.

To ensure smooth processing for both STO and service procurement scenarios, SAP recommends maintaining two separate Business Partners:

  • One Business Partner with plant assignment for STO processes

  • One Business Partner without plant assignment for service procurement

This setup is aligned with the standard system design in Public Cloud and is not considered a workaround.

Regarding your concern about tracking purchases and sales at plant level — there is no need to manually merge information from the two Business Partners. Reporting in the system is primarily driven by organizational elements such as Plant, Company Code, Profit Center, and Purchasing Organization, rather than the Business Partner number alone.

Therefore:

  • All purchase transactions can be tracked by filtering on the relevant Plant.

  • Intercompany sales and STO-related postings remain visible through standard financial and plant-based reports.

  • Financial reporting and consolidation are not impacted by maintaining two Business Partners.

The separation is technical for process control purposes only and does not affect plant-level visibility or financial reporting.

Please let us know if you would like assistance reviewing your reporting setup to ensure full visibility at plant level.

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Automating Probation Period Extension Using Intelligent Services Center in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi @RISHIKA_R ,

Thanks for writing this very informative and interesting blog. I really appreciate the effort that has gone into explaining the approach. I had a few additional thoughts and scenarios that might be worth considering:

  1. Leave Cancellation Scenario
    What happens when an employee cancels approved leave? My understanding is that an ISC event would be triggered in this case.

    • Would this create an additional dummy Job Information record?
    • If cancelled Employee Time entries are excluded from the Integration Center job filter, would it require manually deleting the corresponding Job Info record?
      It may also be worth checking whether the system can automatically deduct the number of days during cancellation and update the custom field in Employee Time(update the existing rule to include this scenario) , which should then correctly reset the probation end date in Job Information.

  2. Overlapping Leave With Probation End Date
    In cases where a leave period overlaps the current probation end date, we may need to calculate the number of leave days only from the Employee Time start date up to the probation end date. This edge scenario might require to update the existing rule where instead of employee time - end date, you can use probation end date. 

  3. Past-Dated Leave After Probation End Date
    If an employee submits a past-dated leave request after the probation end date(if there is no validation that prevent this action), the current conditions may not adjust the probation end date. You might need to consider comparing the Employee Time start date against the existing probation end date to ensure these cases are handled correctly.

  4. Back-to-Back Transactions
    If an employee cancels two separate leave requests back-to-back - could this impact the robustness of the solution? It may be useful to introduce cancellation workflow if its not already exists?

Regards,

VR

entry.title: Learn How to Build Analytical Queries Using ABAP CDS
entry.content:
The data foundation for SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics is the Virtual Data Model (VDM). The VDM is built using ABAP CDS views that form a stack. The stack starts at the bottom with dimension and fact CDS views reading from ABAP tables. The next layer is the cube that combine the dimensions and measures. Finally we have the analytical queries that are built using a special type of ABAP CDS view called a CDS analytical projection view and they sit at the top layer.

SAP provide thousands of CDS views for all the levels of the stack, out-of-the-box and for all application areas. So why would you want to learn how to build them if SAP already provide them?

Firstly, it is helpful to be able to explore the SAP-supplied CDS views and make sense of them. To do this you need to understand the special syntax that is used. Secondly, you may want to develop your own custom CDS views from scratch for consumption by the analytics tools of SAP S/4HANA. And thirdly, SAP Business Data Cloud consumes ABAP CDS views to generate Data Products. So being familiar with this technology is a very useful skill to have in a BDC project.

In the learning journey Discovering SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics we dip our toe in the water and introduce you to the CDS views of the virtual data model. But we don't go into detail.

For those who want to really dig deep into this topic we developed the learning journey: 
Developing Analytical Queries using ABAP CDS 

In this learning journey you will learn how to to create your own analytical queries using ABAP CDS from scratch. There are lots of videos and code snippets that show you how to express your query requirements.

We even provide you with all the code that is used in the learning journey so you can follow along in your own system. 

As well as the learning journey, we provide the following Live Sessions that bring to life various aspects of analytical query building.

Develop Queries using CDS Analytical Projection Views 

Building Cubes using ABAP CDS Views 

Developing Hierarchies using ABAP CDS 

From Cube to Analytical App in 10 minutes Using the ADT Generator 

We highly recommend you attend these planned Live Sessions (or watch the recordings) to help you quickly develop your skills and to ask questions and learn more about planned enhancements.

Developing analytical queries using ABAP CDS is a hot topic. Many customers need help and this is why we created this learning journey. Remember, the virtual data model of SAP S/4HANA depends on ABAP CDS views. Understanding how they work will ensure you are a much sought after member of your SAP project team.

entry.title: Question bout how to install doc AI and chose editon and version and question about pricing
entry.content:
Hi

I hope the community can help me clarify two Document AI questions.

1) First question is about installation and how to choose edition and version

The documentation is talking a lot about functionality limited to the premium edition, but I have found absolutely no documentation about how to get a premium edition installed.

Basically all I can find about installation is below page. It says noting about base edition vs Premium edition and it says noting about how to install v1 or v2. I assume that I have to choose edition and version?

As far as I can see the boosters are only for Free Tier, so also no help to get there.

2) My second question is about the pricing

I think the pricing is cryptic. Again the pricing is related to the edition and version. From the anti-overview in the documentation I see that base edition is only for version 1 and that premium edition is available in both flavours V1 and V2. So far so good although that primarily brings me back to the first question - how to choose edition and version during installation (Enabling).

I can also see that base edition and premium edition are both metered on blocks of 100 documents. The base editon is additionally metered on "compute hours" where the premium edition is metered on requests and users.

(Here I notice that Premium Edition/Version 1 does not have metering of compute hours and also no metering of "Requsts and Users" - only blocks of documents. I wonder if this is just an error in the documentation?)

So far so complicated and still no idea what the cost is.

The documentation also mention that a block of 100 documents cost about 20 EUR - sometimes a document counts for more than one document, but so far ok understandable and it seems to be a ok price.

1 Compute hour cost 1 EUR - yeehaa that's cheap
1 template activation cost 5 compute hours - ok, then forget about the "yeehaa that's cheap"
1 transaction(document) cost 1 compute second - yeehaa that's very cheep
1 compute hour = 500 compute seconds - wait is an hour not 3600 seconds  - ok, then forget about the "yeehaa that's very cheap"

Then in the middle of the metering and pricing info is a insert stating that by HR the pricing is different :

Back on track with the pricing for the premium edition.

With premium version 1 and 2 now the minimum block size is 500 documents a month and the minimum price is 300 EUR, so this raise the price per document from 20 cent to 60 cent  with a little discount if you load extremely many documents.

 

Finally the premium edition version 2 has an additional metering/pricing for " Requests and Users". If I try to add this to the pricing formular, my head begins to spin, I forget all I just learned and I have to start all over again.

Currently I have no idea how to choose edition and version and I have no idea what the cost would be if I managed to choose a edition and version. The boosters only help to install a free edition, but that is not really what we need.

Best Regards
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Re: FB50 Display only
entry.content: Selecting 'Display' triggers transaction 'FB03', so don't allow user to 'FB50' but to 'FB03'.

entry.title: Re: FB50 Display only
entry.content:
FB50 uses multiple authorization objects. Restricting only one (e.g., ACTVT = 03 in F_BKPF_BUK) is not enough. The user’s role may include another object (like F_BKPF_BLA with ACTVT = 01) that enables posting. SAP checks all relevant objects during posting, so if any allow posting, the transaction goes through.

Solution

  • Run SU53 after the user posts — this shows which authorizations were checked and granted.
  • Check the role in PFCG and adjust all relevant objects:
    • Ensure F_BKPF_AKT is set to ACTVT = 03 only.
    • Remove ACTVT = 01/02 from other objects like F_BKPF_BLA.
  • If you want the user to only display documents:
    • Assign them the role with FB03 (Display Document) instead of FB50.
    • Or restrict FB50 completely and give them display-only transactions.

Normally, all the above activity would be carried out by Security team and you can check with them

entry.title: Re: Configuring SAML 2.0 Authentication for your Secure Login Server
entry.content:
Hi Kalyan,

You can use our SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI solution. Here the authentication is based on an identity provider. By default, this is SAP's Identity Authentication Service but you can also use a third-party identity provider such as Microsoft Entra ID. After successful authentication, the SAP Secure Login Service provisions a short-lived X.509 certificate which can then be used by the Secure Login Client for SSO (SNC) to your ABAP-based systems.

You will find more information about the SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI in SAP Community here:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/single-sign-on

Best regards,

Martina

entry.title: Re: Disable custom action button when a field value chang...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Can you please try the following procedure suggested by AI?

To dynamically enable or disable a custom action button based on a field value change in an SAP Fiori application, you can use the attachChange event on the field in question. This event allows you to listen for changes to the field value and execute a function when the change occurs.

Here is a step-by-step guide to achieve this:

  1. Identify the Field: Determine the field whose value change should trigger the enabling or disabling of the button.

  2. Attach Change Event: Use the attachChange method to listen for changes on the field. This can be done in the onInit method of your controller.

    onInit: function() {
    var oField = this.byId('yourFieldId');
    oField.attachChange(this.onFieldValueChange, this);
},

onFieldValueChange: function(oEvent) {
    var sValue = oEvent.getParameter('newValue');
    var bEnabled = sValue !== ''; // Enable if not blank
    this.byId('idUploadAtt').setEnabled(bEnabled);
}

  3. Update Button State: In the onFieldValueChange function, check the new value of the field. If the value is blank, set the button’s enabled property to false; otherwise, set it to true.

  4. Test: Ensure that the button’s state changes as expected when the field value is modified.

This approach ensures that the button’s enabled state is dynamically updated whenever the field value changes, not just during routing or mode changes.

Kind regards,
Henrike

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
Hello Tobias,
we use simplification items as technical means to determine compatibility package (CP) usage in SAP S/4HANA systems.
Actually, we use them for this purpose since the introduction of the simplification item check framework in 2017.

All simplification items used for compatibility packages are assigned to category "non-strategic-function". 
So, if you have chosen the new CP feature in report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK you will only see CP related items.
For the upgrade use case, you will also find "normal" simplification items with other categories describing deprecations or incompatible changes. If you run a Readiness Check for Upgrade, the simplification items will be displayed in separate tiles in the dashboard.

The fact that the local report does not show the related CP IDs (the 100s and 400s IDs) is a known gap. We currently think about how to mitigate this issue.

Best regards,
Stefan
SAP S/4HANA Product Management

entry.title: Re: Trading partner determination for PGI Material accoun...
entry.content:
This “invalid trading partner” error during PGI in intercompany sales is a classic sign that the trading partner assignment between company codes is inconsistent.

How Trading Partner is determined
Trading partner (field RASSC) is derived from the company code master data (transaction OBY6). Each company code has a field “Trading Partner” where you assign the partner company code. During PGI, the system posts inventory and COGS entries. If the delivering plant belongs to a different company code, the system looks up the trading partner from the company code master of the plant’s company code. If that trading partner points to a different sales org/company code than expected, you’ll see the mismatch you described.

Why you are seeing the wrong Trading Partner
Your sales org 1234 belongs to company code A, but the delivering plant 5678 belongs to company code B. At PGI, the system posts inventory for company code B. The trading partner is pulled from company code B’s master data. If company code B’s trading partner is set incorrectly (e.g., pointing to 3456 instead of 1234), the accounting document will show the “invalid trading partner” error.

How to Check
Go to transaction OBY6 (Company Code Global Data). For the delivering plant’s company code (5678’s company code), check the Trading Partner field. Verify that it points to the correct company code of the selling company (1234’s company code). Do the same for the selling company code — both sides must have consistent trading partner assignments.

entry.title: Re: C_S4CPR – Implementation Consultant: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and Procurement
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: SAP Testing in 2026: Agentic AI and Quality Engineering for S/4HANA & BTP
entry.content:
Quality Engineering Transformation in S/4HANA and BTP Programs

Quality engineering integrated with S/4HANA project architecture is the new norm. It is no longer feasible for a company to simply convert to S/4HANA; they are reinventing their finance, logistics, manufacturing, and procurement processes while transitioning custom logic to BTP and running side-by-side applications. With each architectural choice comes the added complexity of testing for data integrity, service integration, authorization models, and performance characteristics.

In this context, testing begins well before a system is built. Quality engineers are involved in the solution design phase to assess migration paths, extensibility patterns, and potential integration risks. Data conversion strategies should be validated not only for their structural integrity, but also for their business relevance as they relate to S/4HANA’s simplified data model. This can be particularly challenging with historical data. During the build phase, regular configuration changes and transport movements require continuous regression testing rather than milestone-based testing.

Several structural changes define SAP quality engineering at this stage:

  • Testing scope is increasingly derived from business criticality rather than transaction count. Processes with financial exposure or regulatory impact receive deeper validation.
  • BTP extensions require testing beyond SAP GUI transactions. API behavior, event messaging, and cloud security controls must be validated under real usage conditions.
  • Performance and stability testing have shifted earlier in the lifecycle, particularly for hybrid scenarios where S/4HANA interacts with external platforms.

Within this framework, SAP teams are emerging as a practical response to scale. Autonomous systems analyze all configuration changes and provide valuable recommendations for which specific tests should be conducted to achieve maximum test coverage. The SAP Artificial Intelligence team partners with the Quality Engineers to ensure that the intelligence used in testing aligns with the test's business intent. This leads to testing that adapts rather than exhausts test execution, resulting in reduced execution overhead and improved reliability of the S/4HANA and BTP landscapes.

Agentic AI and the Evolution of SAP Testing Models

The adoption of agentic AI introduces a fundamentally different operating model for SAP testing. Automation systems that have been traditionally used, either defined by static logic elements and pre-written scripts or operate in just a 'set and forget' manner. The way that agentic AI is built, on the other hand, is to learn from the outcomes that occur, identify the patterns associated with those outcomes, and act on its own within a pre-defined set of constraints. Automated processes that rely on predetermined scripts to run a series of tests will not work well in SAP systems, as they are constantly changing and closely interrelated.

AI agents in SAP testing environments are capable of monitoring transport activity, configuration updates, and integration changes. When a test fails, these agents evaluate the execution context rather than simply logging an error. In this case, they can determine whether the test failure is due to data inconsistencies, authorization issues, or delays caused by upstream integrations. By reasoning the error context, agents are much more accurate at prioritizing and classifying defects, reducing the need for rework.

Common applications of agentic AI in SAP quality engineering include:

  • Dynamic regression planning where agents adjust test scope based on recent system changes rather than executing full regression cycles.
  • Intelligent failure clustering that groups related defects and identifies systemic issues instead of isolated symptoms.
  • Context-aware test data generation that aligns with real business scenarios, improving validation depth for complex S/4HANA processes.

These capabilities also reshape team structures. Agentic SAP teams operate with a shared responsibility model, where quality engineers define validation objectives, and AI systems handle execution level decisions. SAP AI teams provide governance, ensuring transparency and auditability of autonomous actions. Over time, these systems accumulate knowledge from prior releases, enabling predictive insights that anticipate failure patterns before they impact production.

As enterprises evolve to develop Agentic AI Services, there’s an ever-increasing demand from companies that want to create scalable solutions to incorporate into their existing SAP landscapes, while also minimizing the amount of manual work. If properly implemented, SAP testing programs may realize tangible improvements in release confidence, defect containment, and operating efficiency - all without sacrificing an enterprise’s ability to maintain control.

Conclusion

The defining characteristics of SAP testing in 2026 are depth, continuity, and intelligence. The quality engineering practices required for S/4HANA and BTP programs require an understanding of the technical architecture and business context. Active testing has moved beyond a final check to become an inherent part of the testing discipline, influencing design, observing how it is executed, and facilitating continual improvement in SAP environments.

The rise of agentic AI in SAP testing represents a natural progression in this evolution. By enabling autonomous reasoning and adaptive execution, AI agents (e.g., chatbots) within SAP ecosystems enable organizations to manage the complexity of their operations without increasing testing overhead. As SAP AI teams add these capabilities to SAP, and enterprises use agentic AI services, quality engineering becomes predictive, reactive, and aligned with overarching enterprise transformation goals. In this environment, AI used for SAP testing is no longer an experiment but an operational requirement for maintaining reliable, scalable SAP systems.

entry.title: Re: Question bout how to install doc AI and chose editon...
entry.content:
Good questions and you're definitely not alone in being confused by this, the docs can be a bit all over the place on this topic 😅

The key thing you're probably missing is that there's no separate "edition selection" step during setup. The edition and version are simply determined by which BTP service plan you pick when creating your service instance.

Since you want Premium Edition, forget the Booster as you noticed, it's really only meant for Free Tier. Instead, do it manually:

1. BTP Cockpit → your subaccount → Entitlements → Edit → Add Service Plans → search for SAP Document AI → add the `premium_edition` plan
2. Then go to Service Marketplace → SAP Document AI → Create Instance → pick `premium_edition` as plan

There's a step-by-step tutorial on SAP Developers that walks through exactly this (just swap the `free` plan for `premium_edition` wherever it mentions it):
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-aibus-dox-free-service-instance..html

And for the general Initial Setup, this is your go-to reference:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DOCUMENT_AI/5fa7265b9ff64d73bac7cec61ee55ae6/0d68dc0002f0484ba25f85f3170166e0.html

On the pricing, you've already mentioned most of it yourself! Given the complexity of the Premium V2 metering on top of everything else, I'd honestly just reach out to your SAP account manager at this point. They'll be able to give you a concrete cost estimate based on your actual document volumes, which is going to be way more useful than trying to calculate it from the documentation.

entry.title: 'Function SUMGT is unknown' error in Custom Analytical Query
entry.content:
I am trying to create a Custom analytical query using Custom CDS view for a report. Through Custom analytical query and services, I am using SUMGT for calculated Measure, on which I am getting 'Function SUMGT is unknown' in the error logs. 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 'Function SUMGT is unknown' error in Custom Analytica...
entry.content:
I can see that you have used SUMGT in calculated measure. This is not allowed in Custom analytical query. You can use GRAND_TOTAL as an alternative for SUMGT in this case.

To check the values that are allowed in calculated measure, please click on Functions Button to view the values that are available.
